D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

ColorBrewer Families

match the palette to the data — sequential for magnitude, diverging for deviation, qualitative for categories

Cynthia Brewer's ColorBrewer sorts map palettes into three jobs. Sequential ramps encode ordered magnitude (low to high). Diverging ramps put a neutral midpoint between two opposed directions (below/above a baseline). Qualitative sets give maximally distinct hues for unordered categories.

Switch families to see two example schemes each, with a sample chart below: a gradient for sequential, a centred bar for diverging, distinct category blocks for qualitative. Picking the wrong family is the most common data-viz colour mistake.
